info.title: Cbox Billing API
info.summary: Enforcement + management HTTP API for the Cbox Billing application.
info.description:
The HTTP API of **Cbox Billing** — the self-hostable usage-based billing, metering
and entitlement application built on `cboxdk/laravel-billing`.

Two token-authenticated surfaces share the `/api/v1` prefix, the same bearer-token
auth and the same per-org scope, and differ only in throttle tier:

- **Enforcement API** — the metered hot path (`leases`, `usage`, `reserve`, `commit`,
  `entitlements`) the `cboxdk/laravel-billing-client` SDK consumes on every metered
  operation. Higher throttle (`cbox-enforcement`).
- **Management API** — the self-service surface (plans, organizations, subscriptions,
  seats, usage, invoices, hosted checkout/portal sessions, embedded intents, payment
  methods, on-prem licenses, test clocks). Human-paced, mutating, and — for writes
  that must not double-apply — idempotency-keyed. Lower throttle (`cbox-management`).

A third, unauthenticated surface — the on-prem **license activation** heartbeat
(`GET /license/activate`) — lives under the same `/api/v1` prefix; the opaque
deployment id is its only credential.

## Authentication

Every `/api/v1` call except `license/activate` carries a bearer token:

```http
Authorization: Bearer cbl_live_token_value
```

A token is either **operator** (may act for any org) or **org-scoped** (may act only
for its own org — acting for another org is `403`). The token's plaintext prefix
encodes its billing plane: `cbl_` is **live**, `cbt_` is **test** (sandbox). A test
token sees and writes only the sandbox dataset; the test-clock advance endpoint refuses
a live token.

## Idempotency

Mutating writes that must not double-apply accept an **`Idempotency-Key`** request
header. The first attempt runs and its 2xx response is stored under a hash of the
caller's token; a retry with the same key **replays** that stored response (flagged
`Idempotency-Replayed: true`) instead of re-running the effect. Reusing a key with a
different request body is a `409`. The operations that honour it are marked in this
spec (see each operation's `Idempotency-Key` parameter).

## Rate limits

Limits are per bearer token (client IP is the fallback). Every response carries
`X-RateLimit-Limit` and `X-RateLimit-Remaining`; a `429` additionally carries
`Retry-After`. Defaults: enforcement 600/min, management 60/min.

## Money and time

All monetary amounts are **integer minor units** (e.g. cents/øre) of an ISO-4217
currency; a field named `*_minor` is minor units. Timestamps are ISO-8601 strings.

tags.name: Enforcement
tags.description: The metered hot path — lease, ingest, reserve, commit, entitlements.

tags.name: Organizations
tags.description: The billing account a merchant platform provisions and bills for.

tags.name: Subscriptions
tags.description: Subscription lifecycle — subscribe, preview/change, cancel, pause/resume, reactivate, quantity, add-ons.

tags.name: Usage
tags.description: Per-meter usage-against-allowance for the current period.

tags.name: Checkout & Portal
tags.description: Hosted checkout / customer-portal session creation (returns a hosted URL).

tags.name: Payment intents
tags.description: Embedded setup/payment intents and saved payment methods.

tags.name: Licenses
tags.description: On-prem license issue / renew / revoke (operator-authed) and activation.

post.summary: Lease a slice of an org's remaining allowance
post.description:
Lease up to `size` units of an org's remaining includable allowance for a meter —
billing's side of the pessimistic lease the SDK refills local enforcement from.
Grants fewer than requested when the allowance is nearly spent, `0` when exhausted.

Ingest monotonically-increasing `{meter, cumulative, seq}` readings. Each is
converted to the delta the immutable event log stores and deduplicated by `seq`,
so the SDK may retry freely and billing may reprocess without double-counting.

Reserve a set of meter buckets in one all-or-nothing call. The engine's three-way
outcome is returned verbatim:

- `allowed` — the held set is persisted for the matching `/commit` and its
  `reservation_id` is returned.
- `denied` — a semantic refusal (unknown/disabled meter, exhausted allowance/quota).
  Fail-closed. Carries an `upgrade` offer when a reachable plan would unblock it.
- `indeterminate` — a dependency was down; the deployment's infra policy decided
  admit/refuse and the reason is surfaced.

Note: the HTTP status is always `200` — the decision is in the `outcome` field.

post.summary: Commit a reservation to actual usage
post.description:
Settle a held reservation to the actual usage per meter. Returns unused
allowance/lease and appends one durable usage event per bucket with non-zero usage,
then drops the reservation. A missing/expired reservation is `404`; an actual that
exceeds its reserved estimate is `422` from the engine's own validation.

value.error: Unknown or expired reservation.

get.description:
The org's resolved per-meter policies — the payload the SDK caches to enforce
locally, read through the same policy resolver the enforcer uses. Deny-by-default:
a meter with no resolved policy is reported `enabled: false`, never omitted. A
disabled meter with a reachable upgrade path also carries an `upgrade` offer.

The active catalog, each plan priced in the caller's account currency (or an
explicit `?currency=` for signup, before an account exists). A plan not priced in
the resolved currency is omitted rather than shown at a fabricated rate. A
product-scoped token sees only its own product's plans.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: ISO-4217 currency to price the catalog in. Defaults to the caller's account currency, else the app default.

put.description:
Idempotent upsert of the organization a merchant platform bills for. The org id is
the platform's own tenant key. `billing_currency` participates in the one-way
currency lock — it is applied only on **create**, never rewritten for an existing org.

post.summary: Subscribe an organization to a plan
post.description:
Subscribe the org to a plan. With `trial: true` (or a positive `trial_days`) the
subscription opens in a free trial that converts on the scheduled trial pass. A
`coupon` is validated up front (deny-by-default → `422`) and bound after the
subscription exists. Honours `Idempotency-Key` so a retried subscribe cannot
create a duplicate.

value.error: Unknown plan.
This organization has no active subscription.

200.description: The consequence of the change, uncommitted.

404.description: No active subscription, or unknown plan.

post.summary: Apply a plan change (now or at period end)
post.description:
Apply a plan change now (`when: now`, the default) or schedule it for the current
period end (`when: period_end`). The `scheduled` flag distinguishes the deferred
case. Honours `Idempotency-Key` (a retried charge must not re-prorate).

404.description: No active subscription, or unknown plan.

post.description:
The retention fork. `mode` is `immediate`, `period_end`, or `pause` (a
pause-instead-of-cancel save); when absent it derives from the legacy
`at_period_end` flag (default `true` → period-end). `reason`/`feedback` are captured
for churn analytics regardless of mode.

200.description: The subscription after applying the cancellation.

post.summary: Reactivate a paused / scheduled-cancel / recently-canceled subscription
post.description:
Win-back: resume a paused subscription, undo a scheduled period-end cancel, or
re-activate one canceled within the win-back window. `409` when the subscription is
in none of those reactivatable states.

409.description: The subscription is not in a reactivatable state.

value.error: This subscription cannot be reactivated.

post.description:
Change the seat quantity with preview-equals-charge proration. `preview: true`
computes the consequence without applying it. Honours `Idempotency-Key`.

Attach an add-on (aligned or independent), or compute the prorated consequence with
`preview: true`. Honours `Idempotency-Key`.

200.description: The subscription after detaching the add-on.

404.description: No active subscription, or no such add-on.

value.error: This organization has no such add-on.

post.description:
Set the purchased Full-seat count through the engine's prorated changeQuantity.
Refuses dropping below the assigned count or below one (`409`). Honours
`Idempotency-Key`.

409.description: A refused seat invariant (below assigned count, or below one).

value.error: Cannot release seats below the assigned count.

post.summary: Assign a free purchased seat to a member

409.description: No seat free, or the subject is not eligible.

value.error: No free seat available.

post.summary: Free a member's seat (they become Light)

get.summary: Per-meter usage-against-allowance for the current period
get.description:
The org's per-meter usage for the current billing period: `used` reconciled from
the durable event log, the plan `allowance`, `overage` already run past it, and a
straight-line `projected` end-of-period figure. A meter with an unlimited (null)
allowance carries no overage.

post.description:
Open a hosted checkout session and return the `url` the customer completes payment
at. The plan must be priced in the resolved currency (deny-by-default → `422`). A
`coupon` is validated up front.

post.summary: Create a gateway SetupIntent (save a card off-session)
post.description:
Create a gateway SetupIntent so a product's own frontend can save a card
off-session against the gateway's element. No charge is made; the returned
`client_secret` is what the element confirms against.

Create a gateway PaymentIntent a product's frontend confirms on-session. Either
charge a named `invoice` (amount + currency taken from the invoice) or an ad-hoc
`amount` in minor units of `currency`. Reaching succeeded client-side never settles
anything — the engine marks paid strictly on the settled webhook.

404.description: Unknown organization, or unknown invoice for this organization.

value.error: Unknown invoice for this organization.

get.summary: List an org's saved payment methods
get.description: The non-sensitive display fields only (brand/last4/expiry) — the gateway owns the vault.

post.summary: Make a saved method the off-session default

200.description: The saved methods after setting the default.

post.description:
Mint a signed, offline-verifiable license for a customer on a licensable plan.
Operator-authed (an org-scoped token is `403`). Deny-by-default: a non-licensable
plan is `422`. The response carries the signed `key` artifact (the copy-pasteable
`CBOX_ID_LICENSE_KEY`) — a **show-once secret** that is stripped from any idempotency
replay copy. Honours `Idempotency-Key`.

value.error: This action requires an operator token.

422.description: A non-licensable plan, or a validation error.

value.error: Plan [pro] is not licensable.

post.summary: Renew a license (reissue with an extended window)
Revoke a license (add to the signed revocation list)

get.description:
The optional online activation heartbeat a self-hosted deployment may call to
refresh its artifacts. **Unauthenticated** — the opaque `deployment_id` is the only
credential; an unknown deployment gets a generic `404`. Rate-limited (30/min).
Offline installs never call this. Returns the latest signed license bound to the
deployment, the current signed revocation list, and the issuer public key.

404.description: No license found for this deployment.

value.error: No license found for this deployment.
A deployment_id is required.

post.description:
Fast-forward a test clock's virtual time and run the due billing logic for its bound
test subscriptions, returning the counts of what fired. **Test-mode token only** — a
live credential (`cbl_`) is refused with `403`. This is how an integrator scripts
"simulate a year of renewals" from a test suite or CI job.

value.error: A test-mode API token is required to advance a test clock.
Test clock not found.

bearerAuth.description:
A Cbox Billing API token. The plaintext prefix encodes the billing plane: `cbl_`
(live) or `cbt_` (test/sandbox). Operator tokens may act for any org; org-scoped
tokens only for their own. Provision one with
`php artisan billing:token "<name>" --org=<org-id>`.

OrgPath.name: org
OrgPath.description: The organization id (the merchant platform's own tenant key).

IdempotencyKeyOptional.name: Idempotency-Key

XRateLimitLimit.description: The per-window request ceiling for this token.

XRateLimitRemaining.description: Requests remaining in the current window.

RetryAfter.description: Seconds to wait before retrying (present on 429).

IdempotencyKey.description: Echoes the request's `Idempotency-Key` when the write was idempotency-tracked.

IdempotencyReplayed.description: Present and `true` when this response was replayed from a prior identical request.

value.error: Missing bearer token.
Invalid API token.

Forbidden.description: The token may not act for the requested organization (or lacks operator rights).

value.error: This token is not permitted to act for the requested organization.

IdempotencyConflict.description:
The `Idempotency-Key` was reused with a different request body, or a request with
this key is still in flight.

value.error: This Idempotency-Key was already used with a different request payload.
A request with this Idempotency-Key is already in progress.

UnprocessableEntity.description:
Validation failed, or a business rule refused the request. Field validation returns
Laravel's `{message, errors}` shape; a business refusal (invalid coupon, plan not
priced in the currency, non-licensable plan) returns `{error}`.

value.error: Plan [pro] is not priced in EUR.

TooManyRequests.description: The per-token rate limit was exceeded.

Error.description: The app's canonical error shape for auth, scope, not-found, conflict, and business refusals.

ValidationError.description: Laravel's field-validation error shape (thrown by request validation).

size.description: The number of units to lease.

granted.description: Units actually granted (may be fewer than requested; 0 when exhausted).

seq.description: A monotonic sequence number used for dedup.

ReserveOutcome.description:
The three-way reservation outcome. `outcome` is always present; the remaining
fields depend on it (`reservation_id` when allowed; `reason` + optional `upgrade`
when denied; `reason` when indeterminate).

reservation_id.description: Present when allowed — pass it to `/commit`.

upgrade.description: Present on a denial that a reachable plan would unblock.

UpgradeOffer.description: The path to unlock a blocked meter — the minimum reachable plan and a pre-built checkout deep-link.

Entitlement.description: A resolved per-meter policy. A disabled meter reports `enabled: false`; an unlimited one a null `allowance`.

allowance.description: Included units per period; null when unlimited or disabled.

weight.description: The per-event multiplier; null when disabled.

upgrade.description: Present on a disabled meter that a reachable plan would enable.

minor.description: The amount in integer minor units.

currency.description: The currency the catalog was priced in.

billing_currency.description: ISO-4217. Applied on create only (one-way currency lock).

trial.description: Open the subscription in a free trial.

renews_at.description: Null when set to cancel at period end or paused.

payment_intent.description: Reserved for the payment-intents flow; null for the out-of-band manual gateway.

scheduled.description: True when the change was deferred to period end.

effective_at.description: For a scheduled change, the deferred effective date (overrides the preview's immediate `effective_at`).

PlanChangeResult.description: A `PlanChangePreview` plus a `scheduled` flag. For a scheduled change the top-level `effective_at` is the deferred date.

at_period_end.description: Legacy flag used when `mode` is absent.

applied.description: False when this was a preview.

due_now_minor.description: Charge due now (0 when the change is a credit).

credit_minor.description: Credit issued (0 when the change is a charge).

anchor_day.description: For an independent add-on, the billing anchor day.

AddOnPreview.description: The engine's prorated add-on preview (shape depends on the engine; `due_now_minor` + `currency` are always present).

purchased.description: Purchased Full seats (the subscription quantity — the only billing driver).

full.description: Subject ids holding a Full seat.

url.description: The hosted page URL (the opaque token in it is the authorization).

invoice.description: An invoice number to charge (amount + currency taken from it).

client_secret.description: What the gateway element confirms against.

customer_id.description: The organization id the license is for.

key.description: The signed license artifact — a show-once secret (null in an idempotency replay copy).

public_key.description: The issuer Ed25519 public key for offline verification.

target.description: The virtual time to fast-forward to.
